marketState: neutral-to-cautiously-bullish - hot 8-16D weather and a rising fair-value curve offset a large storage surplus and bearish-trending technicals
summary: NG=F front trades at $2.940, essentially at fair value ($2.917 estimate) but running 10.3pp below its typical seasonal path YTD, with Low-reliability seasonality data showing a modest bearish tilt (probHigher 38/38/50/31% and a typical 4-week move of -3.1%). The standout signal is the 8-16 day weather outlook: demand is forecast 50% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, a bullish catalyst that should help absorb the current 184 Bcf storage surplus (last week's +72 Bcf build was a bearish surprise). The fair-value model itself trends higher over the next three weeks (from $2.917 now to $3.127 by +21D) with the front contract now sitting slightly under that rising curve (SLIGHTLY UNDER at +21D), and managed-money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range, leaving room for short covering. Technicals remain in a broad downtrend structure (EMA50<200 on every timeframe, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) but are oversold on 4h/1d RSI, so near-term direction is unclear. Net: the higher-priority weather and fair-value signals argue for a cautious longer-term long, while short-term technicals and price-vs-fair are too balanced to trade directionally without confirmation.
